hypothesis: Including protein-structure-derived 3D pharmacophore restraints (explicit spatial coordinates of H‑bond donors/acceptors and hydrophobic/aromatic features) in the reinforcement-learning reward will produce generated molecules whose median in vitro binding affinity (Kd) to estrogen receptor alpha is at least two-fold better than molecules generated using CATS-based pharmacophore guidance alone while maintaining equal or greater structural novelty (median MAP4 Tanimoto similarity to the reference set ≤ 0.35).
